reviews.review_description: Better game than regular brick braker but I wish I could finish the daily challenges I didn't finish

This is a fantastic game. Cool graphics, the music is surprisingly good & fits the tempo of the game perfectly. The mechanics are simple and fun, but it's challenging at the same time. You'll progressively get better as you play through the more advanced levels. You can play this comfortably seated too. Even with a bunch of games on my Quest, I find myself going back to Masternoid regularly. A+

Amongst the massive pile of meh that applab has to offer, this brick breaking beauty truly shines! The gameplay is addictive, voxel art is beautiful, soundtracks fantastic and the game doesn't make me sweat. There are 50 levels all with individual global leaderboards, so there is tons of replayability. The game itself feels pretty complete as it is. With that being said I hope the developer continues to add content. Absolutely worth the price of admission and should definitely be in the official quest store.

Loved Arkanoid as a kid and Masternoid captures some of that brick-breaking magic. The music is awesome too. Big thanks to the dev for providing a code to giveaway in my best of App Lab video: https://youtu.be/ZNFV4r8MAYw

Masternoid is a simple but addictive brick-breaker VR game, similar to classics like Arkanoid and Breakout. The old-school gameplay, synth music and retro graphics work very well together in creating an experience that can be both tense and relaxing. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thank you for this great review! Tense and relaxing is exactly what we were aiming for :-)
Thank you! We are very happy that you liked it!

reviews.review_description:
Masternoid recreates the retro arcade feel convincingly, to the point where it feels more like a futuristic 80's game than it does a retro VR game.
First off, the music is sublime.
The game is easy to pick up, and level progression is good. There is some depth with the multi-ball and multiplier mechanics, which isn't too hard to figure out. There is even room for strategy if you're going for high scores.
Systematically clearing a level is a form of therapy. You can just zone out and think about stuff while your reflexes take care of the balls.
This is currently my favourite place to escape to.
To the developers: thanks for an amazing game! Also, $@#! you and your final level ^_^

developer_response.body: Thank for you kind words! We are really happy that you liked it and we managed to convey this 80s vibe! And yes @#! the last level :-D

reviews.review_description: when i first saw it as a project, i said neeeh...it will be another boring shooter, ill try only cause its from Greek devs..but surprisingly i got addicted. Good physics and interaction and lot of replayability.  A very funny and enjoyable game..Μπραβο παιδια

developer_response.body: We are very glad that you enjoyed the game after all! Ευχαριστούμε πολύ! :-)

reviews.review_description:
I'm a sucker for breakout/Arkanoid games and this is fantastic!!  There are some other breakout style games on the Oculus and none has really claimed to be the must-have VR breakout game, but Masternoid could finally be the game to claim the crown.

Hopefully more levels and more weapons will be introduced. Perhaps even the ability to play your own music (which adds replay-ability to all games - I'm still playing Racket NX 2 years later because of this added function!!!)

Great game!!

**An Endless Mode, where you only have 3 lives/balls, the levels keep coming until you lose all the balls (which get faster as you progress!!), would be great!!**

**Having played through all the levels, I have to say this is an absolute joy to play. Once you know how to score the best points, you really start climbing the leaderboards for each level.

There's so much potential here for extra content!!

The icing on the cake would be a level editor where you can create and then share your creations!!

One thing I would say, is that last level, while it made me smile due to its difficulty compared to the other levels, I hope if the developers make more levels, that they don't make too many similar to that last level. While it was a nice change, if there's too many of those levels, the game might turn from a joy to play to frustrating to play!!

11 out of 10!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for this great review and the feedback! We have a lot of ideas for future updates some of them already in the works and more levels is definitely part of our plan!

reviews.review_description:
My video review & gameplay: https://youtu.be/lnmGxVX4_L0

Masternoid is an immersive 3D and VR take on the classic brick-breaking/block-breaking arcade game similar to Arkanoid. With 50 levels to play and global leaderboards, the game is sure to provide hours of fun. 

This game is a must-play for any fan of the classic block-breaking genre! Its amazing, dynamic soundtrack transports you back to the good old days while still keeping the pace of the game fresh and exciting.

The goal of this game is to break all of the voxel bricks, while preventing the ball from passing your position. If the ball does pass your position, you lose a life. You only have three lives, so if you lose them all, it's game over. You need to achieve a certain number of stars to progress to the next set of levels.

This game is super comfortable, and you can play it seated or standing. It has a slower pace compared to games like Racket: Nx, so you won't be sweating with this one. It uses only one hand for the gun, and I really wanted my other hand to do something in this game. Even though it's more accessible, it still plays and feels great.

Although the game didn't quite give me the adrenaline rush I was hoping for, it still delivers a fantastic gameplay experience. I respect the fact that it stays loyal to the pace of the classic arcade games. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thank for for the great review and all the well thought feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Masternoid is pure distilled VR pleasure.
The premise is as classic as gaming, break the bricks, don't lose the ball, get as many points as you can.
Such a blissful experience.
It can be so zen or take intense focus.
One of my favorite VR games of all time.

Easy to grasp, hard to master. If you are a highscore achiever this game is for you. I lost track of time quite a few times by trying to achieve a better highscore, until my Quest's battery gave up on me, forcing me to a break.

Controls of the game are easy to grasp, you may need a few runs to understand how everything works (what gives you better multiplier, what the powers ups do etc) and after that the fun starts. Graphics are quite nice and very suitable for this VR take on the classic. Special mention to the soundtrack is in order, having to handle 3 balls at the same time while listening to the music really gets you pumped up.

Everyone who likes VR arcade games should definitely check it out.

developer_response.body: Thank you for this great review! We are really proud of our soundtrack and we are very happy that you liked it!

reviews.review_description: Back in the olden days, I could play this type of brick breaker game for hours on end. It’s a good thing my Quest battery doesn’t last that long 😅. The colors, the music, the leadership tables: this game is fabulous!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review! We are very happy that we managed to get you addicted like the good old days :-)

reviews.review_description: I played the Demo for a while, and just purchased the Full Version.  It's a lot of fun, and is relaxing, with lots of features that I'm still learning about.  The music is spot-on for the game.  Thanks for offering this game, I enjoy it loads.
